artifacts_policy: The materialized harness, configure tree, build tree, smoke executables, the brender_core_float library, and the generated PPM render output were kept outside this metadata repo. No generated binaries, copied BRender source, private assets, or restricted SDK material are committed.
blockers: the render smoke still requires a 32-bit C target because the unreworked period code stores some pointer-shaped values in 32-bit fields
rendering coverage is limited to a wireframe drawn through the pure-C memory pixelmap dispatch; the assembly softrend rasterizer, shaded triangles, textures, and z-buffering are not exercised
the portable core and host fallback stubs still lack semantic tests for the DOS, driver, and rendering behavior they stand in for
image output is a raw PPM dump of the pixelmap buffer; the native BrPixelmapSave datafile path and drivers are not built yet
command: engine-revival materialize-brender-harness --source-root C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132 --output-root C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-harness-render-smoke-2026-07-03; cmake -S C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-harness-render-smoke-2026-07-03 -B C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-build-render-smoke-2026-07-03 -A Win32 -DBRENDER_SOURCE_DIR=C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132; cmake --build C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-build-render-smoke-2026-07-03 --config Debug --target brender_core_smoke; cmake --build ... --target brender_core_startup_smoke; cmake --build ... --target brender_core_render_smoke; ctest --test-dir C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-build-render-smoke-2026-07-03 -C Debug --output-on-failure
host_platform: Windows local probe with Visual Studio 18 2026 and MSVC 19.50.35721.0, Win32 Debug target
next_actions: add semantic tests for the portable core and memory fallback functions before treating the wireframe path as a production renderer
extend the render smoke toward filled and shaded primitives once the generic renderer path is audited
translate the softrend driver so the hardware-free rasterizer covers triangles, textures, and depth
keep generated binaries and PPM output out of git unless an intentional release package is reviewed
public_notes: This attempt is the first BRender revival milestone that produces an actual rendered image rather than only starting the framework. The generated harness materializes a brender_core_render_smoke target that starts BRender, allocates a BR_PMT_RGB_888 memory pixelmap, builds a real BrMatrix4Perspective projection, transforms a unit cube through BrMatrix4Mul and BrMatrix4ApplyP, draws the twelve edges with BrPixelmapLine, reads pixels back with BrPixelmapPixelGet to self-verify, and dumps a 320x240 PPM. Every plotting call flows through the pure-C memory pixelmap dispatch in core/pixelmap/pmmem.c backed by the harness _Mem* primitives; the assembly softrend driver is not used. The program produced 1051 lit pixels and passed as CTest case 3 of 3 under a Visual Studio Win32 Debug target. It does not claim x64 portability, shaded or textured rendering, the native datafile save path, drivers, warning cleanup, or release packaging.
result_summary: The guarded harness configured with -A Win32, built brender_core_float.lib plus all three smoke executables, direct-ran brender_core_render_smoke.exe with exit 0 (320x240 RGB_888, 1051 lit pixels, eight cube vertices projected inside the viewport, PPM written), and passed 3/3 CTest cases with -C Debug.
